components.name: Accordion
components.description: A vertically stacked set of interactive headings that each reveal a section of content. Uses native HTML <details> and <summary> elements with CSS animations.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Is it accessible?
Yes. It adheres to the WAI-ARIA design pattern.
Is it styled?
Yes. It comes with default styles using Tailwind CSS.

components.accessibility_notes: Uses native <details>/<summary> elements for built-in keyboard and screen reader support.

components.name: Alert
components.description: Displays a callout for user attention with optional icon, title, and description.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Default alert with icon, title, and description
variants.html:
Success! Your changes have been saved

variants.name: destructive

variants.name: no-icon
variants.html:
Success! Your changes have been saved

components.accessibility_notes: Structure: <div class="alert"> wraps <svg> (optional icon), <h2> (title), <section> (optional description).

components.name: Alert Dialog
components.description: A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content and expects a response. Unlike Dialog, it has no close button and cannot be dismissed by clicking the backdrop.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Are you absolutely sure?

This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete your account and remove your data from our servers.

components.accessibility_notes: Uses native <dialog> with showModal(). Set aria-labelledby and aria-describedby. No backdrop click dismiss, no close button — forces user decision.

components.name: Avatar
components.description: An image element with a fallback for representing the user. Uses Tailwind utility classes — no dedicated Basecoat class.

variants.name: default
group

components.accessibility_notes: Always include a meaningful alt attribute on avatar images.

</button>

<button class="btn">
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect width="20" height="16" x="2" y="4" rx="2" /><path d="m22 7-8.97 5.7a1.94 1.94 0 0 1-2.06 0L2 7" /></svg>

components.accessibility_notes: Use <button> for actions and <a> for navigation. Combine variant + size classes: e.g. btn-lg-destructive, btn-sm-icon-outline.

variants.name: default

components.name: Dialog
components.description: A window overlaid on either the primary window or another dialog window, rendering the content underneath inert.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Edit profile

Make changes to your profile here. Click save when you're done.

components.accessibility_notes: Uses native <dialog> with showModal(). Set aria-labelledby and aria-describedby. Close on backdrop click with onclick handler.

GitHub
Support
API

components.name: Field
components.description: A form field wrapper that groups a label, input, and description/error message together.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Form field with label, input, and description
variants.html: This is your public display name.

variants.name: invalid
variants.html: Please enter a valid email address.

components.accessibility_notes: Use aria-invalid="true" on inputs with errors. Connect labels with for/id attributes.

components.name: Form
components.description: Form container that automatically styles child <label>, <input>, <textarea>, and <select> elements. Adding the 'form' class to a parent applies styling to all nested form elements.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Complete form with various field types
variants.html: This is your public display name.

components.accessibility_notes: The 'form' class auto-styles child label, input, textarea, and select elements. Use for/id to connect labels.

components.name: Input
components.description: Displays a form input field. Add the 'input' class to style it, or use within a parent with 'form' class.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <input class="input" type="email" placeholder="Email">

variants.name: with-label
with-description
variants.description: Input with label and helper text
variants.html: Fill in your full name.

variants.name: invalid
variants.html: <input class="input" type="email" aria-invalid="true" value="not-an-email">

variants.name: disabled
variants.html: <input class="input" type="text" placeholder="Disabled" disabled>

variants.name: with-button
variants.description: Input paired with a submit button

components.accessibility_notes: Use aria-invalid="true" for error state. Always pair with <label> using for/id. Disabled inputs are excluded from form submission.

components.name: Input Group
components.description: Groups an input with prefix/suffix elements like icons, text, or buttons inside a single visual container.

variants.name: default
with-kbd
variants.description: Input group with keyboard shortcut indicator
variants.html: /

components.accessibility_notes: Input group visually wraps elements. The inner input should still have proper labeling.

components.name: Item
components.description: A versatile list item component for displaying structured content with optional icon, title, description, and actions.

variants.html:

components.accessibility_notes: Use semantic headings inside items. When items are interactive, use appropriate roles.

components.name: Kbd
components.description: Used to display textual user input from keyboard. Shows keyboard shortcuts and key combinations.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <kbd class="kbd">⌘K</kbd>

variants.name: combination
variants.html: Ctrl + B

variants.name: in-button

<button class="btn-outline">

</button>

components.accessibility_notes: Use the <kbd> element for keyboard input. Screen readers will announce it appropriately.

components.name: Label
components.description: Renders an accessible label associated with controls. Style may be affected by disabled peer or child elements.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <label class="label" for="email">Your email address</label>

variants.name: with-input

components.accessibility_notes: Always use the for attribute to connect labels with their inputs. Label styling dims when associated input is disabled.

components.name: Pagination
components.description: Pagination with page navigation, next and previous links. Built with button classes and Tailwind utilities — no dedicated Basecoat class.

variants.description: Pagination with previous, page numbers, and next
variants.html:
• Previous

components.accessibility_notes: Use <nav> with role="navigation" and aria-label="pagination". Current page should use btn-icon-outline to indicate active state.

components.name: Popover
components.description: Displays rich content in a portal, triggered by a button. Supports side and alignment positioning.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Dimensions

Set the dimensions for the layer.

components.accessibility_notes: Uses data-popover attribute. Position with data-side (top/bottom/left/right) and data-align (start/center/end). Requires basecoat.min.js + popover.min.js.

components.name: Progress
components.description: Displays an indicator showing the completion progress of a task. Uses Tailwind utility classes — no dedicated Basecoat class.

variants.name: default
with-label
variants.html: Progress 66%

components.accessibility_notes: For accessible progress, add role="progressbar", aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax attributes.

components.name: Radio Group
components.description: A set of checkable buttons where no more than one can be checked at a time.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
<fieldset class="grid gap-3">
  <label class="label"><input type="radio" name="radio-group" value="default" class="input">Default</label>
  <label class="label"><input type="radio" name="radio-group" value="comfortable" class="input" checked>Comfortable</label>
  <label class="label"><input type="radio" name="radio-group" value="compact" class="input">Compact</label>
</fieldset>

variants.name: with-heading

components.accessibility_notes: Use <fieldset> to group radios. All radios in a group share the same name attribute. Use 'input' class on each radio.

components.name: Select
components.description: Displays a list of options for the user to pick from — triggered by a button. Supports both native HTML select and enhanced JS version.

variants.name: native
variants.html:
<select class="select w-[180px]">
  <optgroup label="Fruits">
    <option>Apple</option>
    <option>Banana</option>
    <option>Blueberry</option>
    <option>Grapes</option>
    <option>Pineapple</option>
  </optgroup>
</select>

variants.name: enhanced
variants.html:
Fruits
Apple
Banana
Blueberry

components.accessibility_notes: Native: add 'select' class to <select>. Enhanced: uses listbox/option ARIA pattern. Requires basecoat.min.js + select.min.js. Supports multiselect via aria-multiselectable="true".

components.name: Sidebar
components.description: A composable, themeable, and customizable sidebar component for navigation. Supports collapsible groups.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Application

Platform
Dashboard Calendar

components.accessibility_notes: Use <aside> with <nav> for sidebar navigation. Requires basecoat.min.js + sidebar.min.js for collapse behavior.

components.name: Skeleton
components.description: Used to show a placeholder while content is loading. Uses animate-pulse Tailwind utility — no dedicated Basecoat class.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Basic skeleton with avatar and text lines

variants.name: card

components.accessibility_notes: Add aria-busy="true" to the loading container and aria-hidden="true" to skeleton elements.

components.name: Slider
components.description: An input where the user selects a value from within a given range. Uses native range input with 'input' class.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <input type="range" class="input w-full" min="0" max="100" value="50">

variants.name: with-js
variants.description: Range slider with JavaScript for painted track
variants.html:
<input type="range" class="input w-full" min="0" max="100" value="50" id="my-slider">
<script>
  (() => {
    const slider = document.getElementById('my-slider');
    const update = (el) => {
      const percent = ((el.value - el.min) / (el.max - el.min)) * 100;
      el.style.setProperty('--slider-value', `${percent}%`);
    };
    update(slider);
    slider.addEventListener('input', (e) => update(e.target));
  })();
</script>

components.accessibility_notes: Uses native <input type="range">. Add min, max, and value attributes. Needs JavaScript to paint the left side of the slider track.

components.name: Spinner
components.description: An indicator that can be used to show a loading state. Uses Lucide loader-circle icon with animate-spin — no dedicated Basecoat class.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="animate-spin" role="status" aria-label="Loading">
  <path d="M21 12a9 9 0 1 1-6.219-8.56" />
</svg>

variants.name: with-text
variants.html: Loading...

variants.name: in-button
variants.html:
<button class="btn" disabled>
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="animate-spin">
    <path d="M21 12a9 9 0 1 1-6.219-8.56" />
  </svg>
  Please wait
</button>

components.accessibility_notes: Add role="status" and aria-label="Loading" to the spinner SVG. Use size-* for different sizes, text-* for colors.

components.name: Switch
components.description: A control that allows the user to toggle between checked and not checked. Uses checkbox with role="switch".

variants.name: default
variants.html: <input type="checkbox" role="switch" class="input">

variants.name: with-label
variants.html: Receive emails about new products, features, and more.

components.accessibility_notes: Use <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> with 'input' class. The role="switch" attribute is required.

components.name: Table
components.description: A responsive table component. Simply add the 'table' class to your table element.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Data table with header and rows
variants.html:
Invoice Status Method Amount
INV001 Paid Credit Card $250.00
INV002 Pending PayPal $150.00
INV003 Unpaid Bank Transfer $350.00
Total $750.00

components.accessibility_notes: Use semantic <thead>, <tbody>, <tfoot>. Add scope attributes to headers for screen readers.

components.name: Tabs
components.description: A set of layered sections of content — known as tab panels — that are displayed one at a time.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
Account

Make changes to your account here.

Password

Change your password here.

components.accessibility_notes: Uses ARIA tab pattern: role="tablist", role="tab", role="tabpanel". Requires basecoat.min.js + tabs.min.js. Connect tabs and panels with aria-controls/aria-labelledby.

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Displays a form textarea. Add the 'textarea' class to style it, or use within a parent with 'form' class.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <textarea class="textarea" placeholder="Type your message here"></textarea>

variants.name: with-label
variants.html: You can @mention other users and organizations.

variants.name: invalid
variants.html: <textarea class="textarea" aria-invalid="true">Invalid content</textarea>

components.accessibility_notes: Use aria-invalid="true" for error state. Always pair with <label>.

components.name: Theme Switcher
components.description: A toggle for switching between light and dark themes. Uses a dropdown or button to change the [data-theme] attribute.

variants.name: default
variants.html:
<button class="btn-icon-ghost" onclick="document.documentElement.dataset.theme = document.documentElement.dataset.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark'" aria-label="Toggle theme">
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="dark:hidden"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="4" /><path d="M12 2v2" /><path d="M12 20v2" /><path d="m4.93 4.93 1.41 1.41" /><path d="m17.66 17.66 1.41 1.41" /><path d="M2 12h2" /><path d="M20 12h2" /><path d="m6.34 17.66-1.41 1.41" /><path d="m19.07 4.93-1.41 1.41" /></svg>
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="hidden dark:block"><path d="M12 3a6 6 0 0 0 9 9 9 9 0 1 1-9-9Z" /></svg>
</button>

components.accessibility_notes: Use aria-label on the toggle button. Set [data-theme] on <html> element to switch themes.

components.name: Toast
components.description: A succinct message that is displayed temporarily. Supports categories: success, info, warning, error.

variants.name: container
variants.description: Toast container (toaster) — place at end of <body>

variants.name: server-rendered
variants.html:
Success

Your changes have been saved.

variants.name: javascript
variants.html: <button class="btn-outline" onclick="document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('basecoat:toast', { detail: { config: { category: 'success', title: 'Success', description: 'Your changes have been saved.', cancel: { label: 'Dismiss' } } } }))">Show Toast</button>

components.accessibility_notes: Uses role="status" and aria-atomic="true". Categories: success, info, warning, error. Default duration 3s (5s for errors). Requires basecoat.min.js + toast.min.js. Place <div id="toaster" class="toaster"> at end of <body>.

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: A popup that displays information related to an element when it receives keyboard focus or the mouse hovers over it. Uses data-tooltip attribute — CSS only.

variants.name: default
variants.html: <button class="btn-outline" data-tooltip="This is a tooltip" data-side="bottom" data-align="center">Hover me</button>

variants.name: top
variants.html: <button class="btn-outline" data-tooltip="Top tooltip" data-side="top">Top</button>

variants.name: right
variants.html: <button class="btn-outline" data-tooltip="Right tooltip" data-side="right">Right</button>

variants.name: left
variants.html: <button class="btn-outline" data-tooltip="Left tooltip" data-side="left">Left</button>

components.accessibility_notes: Set data-tooltip for text, data-side for position (top/bottom/left/right), data-align for alignment (start/center/end). Pure CSS, no JS required.
